Q.36Let M be a $3 \times 3$ real symmetric matrix with eigenvalues -1,1,2 and thecorresponding unit eigenvectors u, v, w, respectively. Let x and y be two vectors in $\mathbb { R } ^ { 3 }$ such thatMx = u + 2(v + w) and M²y = u − (v + 2w).Considering the usual inner product in ${ \mathbb { R } } ^ { 3 }$ , the value of $| x + y | ^ { 2 }$ , where $| x + y |$ isthe length of the vector $x + y ,$ is
(A)1.25
(B)0.25
(C)0.75
(D)1
Q.37Consider the following infinite series: $S _ { 1 } { : = } \sum _ { n = 0 } ^ { \infty } ( - 1 ) ^ { n } { \frac { n } { n ^ { 2 } + 4 } }$ $S _ { 2 } { \mathrel { : } } = \sum _ { n = 0 } ^ { \infty } ( - 1 ) ^ { n } \left( { \sqrt { n ^ { 2 } + 1 } } - n \right) .$ andWhich of the above series is/are conditionally convergent?
(A) $S _ { 1 }$ only
(B) $S _ { 2 }$ only
(C)Both $S _ { 1 }$ and $S _ { 2 }$
(D)Neither $S _ { 1 }$ nor $S _ { 2 }$
Q.38Let $( 3 , 6 ) ^ { T } , ( 4 , 4 ) ^ { T } , ( 5 , 7 ) ^ { T }$ and $( 4 , 7 ) ^ { T }$ be four independent observations from abivariate normal distribution with the mean vector μ and the covariance matrix Σ. Let $\widehat { \pmb { \mu } }$ and $\widehat { \pmb { \Sigma } }$ be the maximum likelihood estimates of $\pmb { \mu }$ and Σ, respectively, based onthese observations. Then $\widehat { \pmb { \Sigma } } \widehat { \pmb { \mu } }$ is equal to
(A) $\textstyle { \binom { 3 . 5 } { 1 0 } }$
(B) $\textstyle { \binom { 7 . 5 } { 4 } }$
(C) $\scriptstyle { \binom { 4 } { 1 3 . 5 } }$
(D) $\textstyle { \binom { 1 0 } { 3 . 5 } }$
Q.39 $\pmb { X } = \left( \begin{array} { l } { X _ { 1 } } \\ { X _ { 2 } } \\ { X _ { 3 } } \end{array} \right)$ ${ \pmb \mu } = \left( - 3 \atop 2 \right)$ ${ \pmb { \Sigma } } = \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 4 } & { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 2 } & { a } \\ { 1 } & { a } & { 2 } \end{array} \right]$ Letfollow $\mathrm { N } _ { 3 } ( { \pmb { \mu } } , { \pmb { \Sigma } } )$ withand, where $a \in \mathbb { R }$ Suppose that the partial correlation coefficient between $X _ { 2 }$ and $X _ { 3 }$ , keeping $X _ { 1 }$ fixed, is $\frac { 5 } { 7 }$ . Then a is equal to
(A)1
(B) $\frac { 3 } { 2 }$
(C)2
(D) $\frac 1 2$
Q.40If the line $y = \alpha x , \alpha \geq { \sqrt { 2 } } .$ divides the area of the region $R \colon = \{ ( x , y ) \in \mathbb { R } ^ { 2 } | 0 \leq x \leq \sqrt { y } , 0 \leq y \leq 2 \}$ into two equal parts, then the value of α is equal to
(A) $\frac { 3 } { \sqrt { 2 } }$
(B) $2 \sqrt { 2 }$
(C) $\sqrt { 2 }$
(D) $\frac { 5 } { 2 { \sqrt { 2 } } }$
Q.41Let (X, Y, Z) be a random vector with the joint probability density function $f _ { X , Y , Z } ( x , y , z ) = \left\{ \begin{array} { l l } { \displaystyle \frac { 1 } { 3 } ( 2 x + 3 y + z ) , \quad } & { 0 < x < 1 , 0 < y < 1 , 0 < z < 1 , } \\ { \displaystyle 0 , \quad } & { \mathrm { e l s e w h e r e . } } \end{array} \right.$ Then which one of the following points is on the regression surface of X on $\left( Y , Z \right) ?$
(A) $\left( \frac { 4 } { 7 } , \frac { 1 } { 3 } , \frac { 1 } { 3 } \right)$
(B) $\left( \frac { 6 } { 7 } , \frac { 2 } { 3 } , \frac { 2 } { 3 } \right)$
(C) $\left( { \frac { 1 } { 2 } } , { \frac { 1 } { 3 } } , { \frac { 2 } { 3 } } \right)$
(D) $\left( \frac { 1 } { 2 } , \frac { 2 } { 3 } , \frac { 1 } { 3 } \right)$

$\operatorname { I f } { \frac { X } { \theta } }$ is used as a pivot for obtaining the confidence interval for $f ( x ; \theta ) = \left\{ { \begin{array} { l l } { 2 x } \\ { \theta ^ { 2 } } , \qquad } & { 0 < x < \theta , } \\ { 0 , } & { { \mathrm { e l s e w h e r e . } } } \end{array} \right.$
decimal places) for θ based on the observed sample valuethe following is an 80% confidence interval (confidence limits rounded off to three $x = 1 0 \mathrm { : }$ $\theta ,$
(A)(10.541, 31.623)

Q.43 Let $X _ { 1 } , X _ { 2 } , . . . , X _ { 7 }$ variancebe a random sample from a normal population with mean 0 and $\theta > 0$ . Let
$K = { \frac { X _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } + X _ { 2 } ^ { 2 } } { X _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } + X _ { 2 } ^ { 2 } + \cdots + X _ { 7 } ^ { 2 } } } .$
Consider the following statements:
(I)The statistics K and $X _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } + X _ { 2 } ^ { 2 } + \cdots + X _ { 7 } ^ { 2 }$ are independent.
(II) $\frac { 7 K } { 2 }$ has an F-distribution with 2 and 7 degrees of freedom.
(III) $\begin{array} { r } { E ( K ^ { 2 } ) = \frac { 8 } { 6 3 } } \end{array}$
Then which of the above statements is/are true?
(A)(I) and (II) only
(B)(I) and (III) only
(C)(II) and (III) only
(D)(I) only
Q.44Consider the following statements:
(I)Let a random variable X have the probability density function
$f _ { X } ( x ) = { \frac { 1 } { 2 } } e ^ { - | x | } , \qquad - \infty < x < \infty .$
Then there exist i.i.d. random variables $X _ { 1 }$ and $X _ { 2 }$ such that X and $X _ { 1 } - X _ { 2 }$ have the same distribution. Let a random variable Y have the probability density function
$f _ { Y } ( y ) = \left\{ { \frac { 1 } { 4 } } , \qquad - 2 < y < 2 , \atop { 0 , } \right.$ Then there exist i.i.d. random variables $Y _ { 1 }$ $Y _ { 1 } - Y _ { 2 }$ have the same distribution. Then which of the above statements is/are true?
(A)(I) only
(B)(II) only
(C) (D)Both (I) and (II) Neither (I) nor (II)
Q.45Suppose $X _ { 1 } , \ X _ { 2 } , \dots , X _ { n } , \dots$ are independent exponential random variables with the $\mathrm { m e a n } \ { \textstyle { \frac { 1 } { 2 } } }$ . Let the notation i. o. denote infinitely often'. Then which of the followingis/are true?
(A) $\begin{array} { r } { P \left( \left\{ X _ { n } > \frac { \epsilon } { 2 } \mathrm { l o g } _ { e } n \right\} i . o . \right) = 1 \mathrm { f o r } 0 < \epsilon \leq 1 } \end{array}$
(B) $\begin{array} { r } { P \left( \left\{ X _ { n } < \frac { \epsilon } { 2 } \mathrm { l o g } _ { e } n \right\} i . o . \right) = 1 \mathrm { f o r } 0 < \epsilon \le 1 } \end{array}$
(C) $\begin{array} { r } { P \left( \left\{ X _ { n } > \frac { \epsilon } { 2 } \mathrm { l o g } _ { e } n \right\} i . o . \right) = 1 \mathrm { f o r } \epsilon > 1 } \end{array}$
(D) $\begin{array} { r } { P \left( \left\{ X _ { n } < \frac { \epsilon } { 2 } \mathrm { l o g } _ { e } n \right\} i . o . \right) = 1 \mathrm { f o r } \epsilon > 1 } \end{array}$
Q.46 Let $\{ X _ { n } \} , n \geq 1$ , be a sequence of random variables with the probability mass functions
$p _ { X _ { n } } ( x ) = \left\{ \begin{array} { l l } { \displaystyle \frac { n } { n + 1 } , } \\ { \displaystyle \frac { 1 } { n + 1 } , } \\ { \displaystyle 0 , } \end{array} \right.$ $x = 0 ,$
$x = n ,$
elsewhere.
(A) $X _ { n }$ converges to X in distribution
(B) $X _ { n }$ converges to X in probability
(C) $E ( X _ { n } ) \longrightarrow E ( X )$
(D)There exists a subsequence $\{ X _ { n _ { k } } \}$ of $\{ X _ { n } \}$ such that $X _ { n _ { k } }$ converges to X almost surely
Q.47Let M be any 3 × 3 symmetric matrix with eigenvalues 1,2 and 3. Let N be any3 × 3 matrix with real eigenvalues such that $\pmb { M } \pmb { N } + \pmb { N } ^ { T } \pmb { M } = 3 \pmb { I } ,$ where I is the $3 \times 3$ identity matrix. Then which of the following cannot be eigenvalue(s) of thematrix N ?
(A) $\frac 1 4$
(B) $\frac 3 4$
(C) $\frac 1 2$
(D) $\frac { 7 } { 4 }$
Q.38Let M be a 3 × 2 real matrix having a singular value decomposition as $\pmb { M } = \pmb { U } \pmb { S } \pmb { V } ^ { T }$ $\pmb { S } = \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { \sqrt { 3 } } & { 0 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right] ^ { T }$ where the matrix, U is a $3 \times 3$ orthogonal matrix, and V is a $2 \times 2$ orthogonal matrix. Then which of the following statements is/are true?
(A)The rank of the matrix M is 1
(B)The trace of the matrix $M ^ { T } M$ is 4
(C)The largest singular value of the matrix $( \pmb { M } ^ { T } \pmb { M } ) ^ { - 1 } \pmb { M } ^ { T }$ is 1
(D)The nullity of the matrix M is 1
Q.49Let X be a random variable such that $P \left( { \frac { a } { 2 \pi } } X \in \mathbb { Z } \right) = 1 , a > 0 ,$ where Z denotes the set of all integers. If $\phi _ { X } ( t ) , t \in \mathbb { R }$ , denotes the characteristicfunction of X, then which of the following is/are true?
(A) $\phi _ { X } ( a ) = 1$
(B) $\phi _ { X } ( \cdot )$ is periodic with period a
(C) $| \phi _ { X } ( t ) | < 1$ for all $t \neq a$
(D) $\begin{array} { r } { \int _ { 0 } ^ { 2 \pi } e ^ { - i t n } \phi _ { X } ( t ) d t = \pi P \left( X = \frac { 2 \pi n } { a } \right) , n \in \mathbb { Z } , \ i = \sqrt { - 1 } } \end{array}$
Q.50Which of the following real valued functions is/are uniformly continuous on $[ 0 , \infty ) ?$
(A) $\sin ^ { 2 } x$
(B)x sin x
(C)sin(sin x)
(D)sin(x sin x)
